There is easy access to local facilities.

the impact on the environment

sections.title: Part 3
sections.context: Talk on the Laki eruption of 1783

questionGroups.instructions: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.question: Why do the students think the Laki eruption of 1783 is so important?

options.text: It was the most severe eruption in modern times.
It led to the formal study of volcanoes.
It had a profound effect on society.

questions.question: What surprised Adam about observations made at the time?

options.text: the number of places producing them
the contradictions in them
the lack of scientific data to support them

questions.question: According to Michelle, what did the contemporary sources say about the Laki haze?

options.text: People thought it was similar to ordinary fog.
It was associated with health issues.
It completely blocked out the sun for weeks.

questions.question: Adam corrects Michelle when she claims that Benjamin Franklin

options.text: came to the wrong conclusion about the cause of the haze.
was the first to identify the reason for the haze.
supported the opinions of other observers about the haze.

questionGroups.question: Which TWO issues following the Laki eruption surprised the students?

options.text: how widespread the effects were
how long-lasting the effects were
the number of deaths it caused
the speed at which the volcanic ash cloud spread
how people ignored the warning signs

questionGroups.instructions: What comment do the students make about the impact of the Laki eruption on the following countries? Choose FOUR answers from the box and write the correct letter, A–F, next to Questions 27–30.

options.text: This country suffered the most severe loss of life.
The impact on agriculture was predictable.
There was a significant increase in deaths of young people.
Animals suffered from a sickness.
This country saw the highest rise in food prices in the world.
It caused a particularly harsh winter.

items: • Women's pockets were less 35 ______ than men's.

questions.question: They are ______ but can be overlooked by consumers and designers.
Men started to wear ______ in the 18th century.
A ______ sewed pockets into the lining of the garments.
Bigger pockets might be made for men who belonged to a certain type of ______.
Women's pockets were less ______ than men's.
Pockets were produced in pairs using ______ to link them together.
Pockets hung from the women's ______ under skirts and petticoats.

When dresses changed shape, hidden pockets had a negative effect on the ______ of women.
Bags called 'pouches' became popular, before women carried a ______.
